old_claim.note: Geometry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No geometric construction, projection, coordinate framework, or angle measurement is documented for the scoped Babylonian World Map.

source_shape.source_locator: Object 1882,0714.509, Description: represents the world as a disc surrounded by the Bitter River, uses simple marks for cities and regions, and places some geography explicitly incorrectly; no geometric method or projection is attributed.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The artifact record supports symbolic spatial arrangement but not a geometry-based construction method.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A round boundary and drawn shapes do not by themselves establish geometry as a causal prerequisite; the former edge converted visual form into an unsupported technical dependency.

ontology_before: The broad node inferred geometry from the fact that the artifact contains shapes and spatial arrangement.
ontology_after: The scoped artifact can be represented as an early map without asserting a formal geometric construction method.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-babylonian-world-map-boundary: geometry is not a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: Authoritative technical study demonstrates a specific geometric construction or projection used on tablet 1882,0714.509.
short_reason: The source documents schematic shapes, not a formal geometric method.
